title: Fix bootstrap non-sync actions to honor server/external mode
description: Sub-agent audit found that bootstrap planning can detect server/external mode, but executeInitAction, executeRestoreAction, and executeJSONLImportAction build a dolt.Config without ServerMode or server endpoint fields. That can make non-sync bootstrap actions create/use .beads/embeddeddolt instead of the configured server database. Add a shared bootstrap dolt.Config builder from configfile.Config, use it in non-sync actions, and cover server/external execution paths.

title: Resolve bd-main Beads remote divergence
description: Why this issue exists: the assessment observed bd stats in bd-main attempting an auto-push that failed because local and remote Dolt histories had no common ancestor. What needs to be done: decide authoritative history, recover with bootstrap or force-push as appropriate, document the choice, and verify bd-main issue tracking can push and pull cleanly.
close_reason: Active bd_main Dolt remote divergence is no longer present: main and origin/main match, bd dolt push succeeds, and the previous divergent local DB/export are preserved for audit in follow-up mybd-75c.1.

title: Harden admin/status/recovery commands across embedded and server modes
description: Why this issue exists: recovery commands must work exactly when storage is unhealthy, and existing backlog clusters around admin/status embedded-mode and server-mode regressions. What needs to be done: verify bd admin, doctor, backup, bootstrap, dolt status, dolt show, and recovery guidance in embedded and server modes; add regression tests for each mode-specific failure path.

title: Phase 0: stabilize Dolt lifecycle and data safety
description: Why this issue exists: the health assessment identifies Dolt lifecycle and sync behavior as the highest operational risk, including embedded startup, lock lifecycle, recovery, migrations, watcher races, remote divergence, and surprising auto-push behavior. What needs to be done: make recovery/admin commands reliable in embedded and server modes, resolve bd-main sync divergence, keep the short Go suite green, and add scenario coverage before larger refactors.
close_reason: Phase 0 stabilization and recovery slices complete

title: v0.63→1.0.x upgrade: migration backfill silently drops ALTER statements, leaves issues.started_at missing
description:
Related: GH#3363

**Symptom:**
After upgrading from v0.63.0 to v1.0.2, `bd list` fails with:
`Error 1105: column "started_at" could not be found in any table in scope`

**Root cause (internal/storage/schema/schema.go):**

v0.63.x used Go-based migrations that did NOT populate the `schema_migrations` table. When v1.0.x MigrateUp runs against such a DB it sees empty `schema_migrations` + existing `issues` table and falls into `backfillMigrations` (schema.go:135-140), which calls `runMigrations` with `tolerateExisting=true`.

In that path (schema.go:199-203), **every** error from migration SQL is swallowed — not only 'already exists' / 'duplicate column'. Then the version is recorded in `schema_migrations` regardless (line 208). Result: `schema_migrations` reports the migration as applied, even when the ALTER never took effect.

Migration 0027_add_started_at.up.sql bundles two ALTERs in one Exec:
```
ALTER TABLE issues ADD COLUMN started_at DATETIME;
ALTER TABLE wisps ADD COLUMN started_at DATETIME;
```
`EnsureIgnoredTables` (store.go:1082) runs BEFORE migrations and already adds `started_at` to wisps (via the filter="wisps" entry in ignored_tables.go:30). The wisps ALTER then errors with duplicate column. Depending on Dolt's multi-statement driver behavior, the preceding `ALTER issues` may be aborted by the batch error. Backfill swallows it. Version 27 is recorded. `issues.started_at` is missing.

Also reported by user (gh issue 3363 thread):
- `bd doctor` reports local_metadata missing (Error 1146) — secondary symptom suggesting working-set state drift in the shared-server path.

**Fix plan:**

1. In `runMigrations` backfill mode, only swallow errors that match `isConcurrentInitError` (already-exists / duplicate column / etc.). Real DDL failures should abort and NOT record the version.
2. Split 0027 into two single-statement migration files (0027a_issues, 0027b_wisps) so a wisps failure can't shadow the issues ALTER. Add a per-statement execution path to MigrateUp as a defensive second layer.
3. Add a post-migration sanity check: probe `information_schema.columns` for the known-added columns (issues.started_at, issues.no_history if applicable, etc.). If missing, attempt idempotent repair and log loudly.
4. Regression test: simulate the v0.63→v1.0 upgrade — create a DB with schema_migrations empty and issues table lacking started_at, run MigrateUp through EnsureIgnoredTables path, assert issues.started_at exists and bd list succeeds.

**Acceptance:**
- `bd list` succeeds immediately after first `bd dolt start` following upgrade from v0.63.x.
- No silent swallowing of non-concurrent DDL errors.
- Schema_migrations accurately reflects applied state.
close_reason: Fixed migration backfill error handling and added regression coverage

comments.author: maphew
comments.text: Fixed on beads branch codex/phase0-integrated: runMigrations no longer has a backfill mode that swallows arbitrary statement errors. Embedded SQL migrations still execute per statement and tolerate only concurrent/already-exists style errors, so real DDL failures abort before schema_migrations records the version. Added schema regression tests for non-concurrent DDL failure, already-exists tolerance, and migration 0027 issues/wisps started_at statements executing independently. Validation: go test -tags gms_pure_go ./internal/storage/schema; go test -tags gms_pure_go ./internal/storage/dolt/migrations ./internal/storage/dolt ./internal/storage/embeddeddolt; git diff --check; GOTMPDIR=/var/home/matt/.cache/codex-go-tmp go test -tags gms_pure_go -short -skip '^TestEmbedded' ./...

title: Add Dolt lifecycle scenario tests
description: Why this issue exists: the highest-risk bugs involve cross-command scenarios rather than isolated functions. What needs to be done: add scenario tests for bootstrap, stale server state, embedded lock contention, remote divergence, migration retry, watcher race prevention, and recovery command availability.

title: Define Dolt lifecycle state model
description: Why this issue exists: startup, migration, lock contention, remote divergence, server availability, and recovery are currently represented by scattered checks. What needs to be done: define explicit states including uninitialized, initialized embedded, initialized server, server unavailable, migration required, migration failed, lock held, lock contended, remote configured, remote diverged, and recovery required.

title: Map doctor/status/recovery output to lifecycle states
description: Why this issue exists: users need deterministic recovery guidance when storage is unhealthy. What needs to be done: centralize state detection and guidance so doctor, dolt status/show, bootstrap, and recovery messages are driven by the lifecycle model rather than separate conditionals.

title: Add server and embedded storage contract tests
description: Why this issue exists: embedded and server Dolt modes need identical behavior for core issue operations but have different lifecycle constraints. What needs to be done: add contract tests for ready/list/show/create/update/close, dependencies, labels, comments, and config behavior that run against both storage backends where applicable.

title: Split DoltStorage use sites into narrow capability interfaces
description: Why this issue exists: broad storage interfaces increase blast radius by giving read-only commands access to remote sync, compaction, federation, and maintenance methods they do not need. What needs to be done: introduce narrow interfaces such as IssueReader, IssueWriter, DependencyStore, ConfigStore, VersionedStore, RemoteSyncStore, and MaintenanceStore at command/service boundaries.

title: Replace direct os.Exit calls below CLI adapter
description: Why this issue exists: many direct os.Exit calls in command code make failure behavior hard to test and make JSON/error contracts harder to preserve. What needs to be done: return typed errors from command services, centralize exit-code mapping in the CLI runner, and migrate dolt/admin paths first.

Preserved existing rendered error output with silent command errors to avoid duplicate Cobra output. Remaining follow-up in this issue: migrate bd dolt set, bd dolt remote subcommands, and admin compact direct exits. Validation: focused cmd/bd tests; git diff --check; GOTMPDIR=/var/home/matt/.cache/codex-go-tmp go test -tags gms_pure_go -short -skip '^TestEmbedded' ./...

comments.author: maphew
comments.text: Second direct-exit migration slice landed on beads branch codex/phase0-integrated: converted bd dolt set and bd dolt remote add/list/remove to return typed command errors instead of calling os.Exit on those paths, while preserving existing rendered error output and JSON error contracts. Remaining follow-up in this issue: admin compact direct exits, which are spread across helper routines and should be converted as a dedicated compact slice. Validation: focused cmd/bd dolt set/remote tests; git diff --check; GOTMPDIR=/var/home/matt/.cache/codex-go-tmp go test -tags gms_pure_go -short -skip '^TestEmbedded' ./...

description: Why this issue exists: bd dolt status/show are recovery-critical and currently sit in command code with direct exits and mode-specific JSON behavior. What needs to be done: create a service with typed inputs/outputs, keep Cobra as the adapter, preserve text output, and add JSON contract tests including embedded server_running semantics.

What needs to be done: ensure read-only commands do not surprise-push or hang, make auto-push policy explicit and bounded, and add tests for unreachable/diverged remotes.

title: Phase 3: model Dolt lifecycle as explicit states
description: Why this issue exists: Dolt startup, bootstrap, migration, locks, auto-start, auto-push, remote divergence, and recovery are currently the highest-risk behaviors and are represented by scattered conditionals. What needs to be done: define lifecycle state detection and transition handling so doctor/status/recovery commands map to deterministic states and guidance.

title: Phase 2: narrow storage capability boundaries
description: Why this issue exists: internal/storage.Storage and DoltStorage expose too broad a surface, making storage changes cross-cutting and forcing embedded and server modes to look identical despite different lifecycle constraints. What needs to be done: introduce narrow use-site interfaces, move shared SQL behavior into issueops where appropriate, and add server/embedded contract tests for capabilities that must match.

title: Phase 1: extract CLI command services
description: Why this issue exists: cmd/bd is the center of gravity, with 613 Go files, transitional globals, command lifecycle state, rendering, process exits, hooks, telemetry, and domain workflows mixed together. What needs to be done: keep Cobra as an adapter while extracting high-churn commands into injectable services with explicit inputs, outputs, dependencies, JSON contracts, and tests.

title: Refactor bd-main from health assessment
description: Why this issue exists: reports/beads-codebase-health-2026-04-24.md concludes that Beads is shippable but stressed, with risk concentrated in Dolt lifecycle, CLI globals, broad storage interfaces, JSON/error contracts, and docs/package drift. What needs to be done: run a staged stabilization and extraction campaign rather than a rewrite, preserving tests and CLI contracts while reducing coupling.

title: Create contributor PR intake checklist for refactoring campaign
description: Why this issue exists: the health assessment recommends protecting contributor work while reducing backlog during the refactoring campaign. What needs to be done: document an intake checklist for reviewing existing PRs before rewriting, identifying clean merges, requesting rebases, preserving tests, and closing superseded work with explicit rationale.

title: Add release-blocking docs and package consistency checks
description: Why this issue exists: docs and packaging drift is easy because command flags, install methods, package metadata, and versioned docs live in many places. What needs to be done: add or extend checks that validate CLI flags, package metadata URLs, version consistency, install snippets, and docs source-of-truth rules during CI/release validation.

title: Normalize canonical repository URLs across packages and docs
description: Why this issue exists: the assessment found docs and metadata still mixing steveyegge/beads and gastownhall/beads references. What needs to be done: normalize README, install scripts, npm package metadata, PyPI metadata, website docs, generated references, and release docs to the intended canonical repository policy.

title: Move shared issue SQL behavior into issueops
description: Why this issue exists: duplicated storage behavior across internal/storage/dolt and internal/storage/embeddeddolt risks drift and inconsistent bug fixes. What needs to be done: identify shared issue CRUD/query/dependency SQL paths and move reusable behavior into issueops without changing external CLI behavior.

title: Extract ready/list/show command behavior behind service APIs
description: Why this issue exists: ready/list/show are common commands with active UX and JSON regressions, and they are good candidates for separating query behavior from Cobra parsing and rendering. What needs to be done: define input/output structs, move query orchestration into services, keep JSON/text rendering separate, and add characterization tests before changing behavior.

title: Phase 4: clean packaging and docs drift
description: Why this issue exists: the project has strong docs and packaging, but many surfaces can drift: README, website, generated CLI references, package metadata, PyPI metadata, install scripts, agent docs, and versioned docs. What needs to be done: normalize canonical repository URLs, declare sources of truth, and add consistency checks to release gates.

title: Clean stale local tool worktrees and track tool dotfiles
description: Why this issue exists: old Claude-created worktrees and stale git worktree records are cluttering the workspace, while useful non-ignored tool dotfiles should be tracked. What needs to be done: remove stale/old worktrees, add .claude command/skill files and .codex, commit, and push.
close_reason: Removed stale worktree entries and prepared non-ignored tool files for tracking
